author: @gen81465
text: The sad part is that since each of these uses part of the name of the channel in it, it's impossible to wholesale block them. I wish YouTube allowed blocking of comments based on keywords. The other thing that scammers like to do is to use email to spoof as if it's from you and ask you to buy a bunch of prepaid debit/credit cards. As an officer in a non-profit, I've had that done to me numerous times. I added the following text to the end of my email signature: "Please note - I will NEVER ask ANYONE to donate money to ANYTHING that isn't legitimate. If you EVER see an email like that and it appears to come from me, contact me immediately and I will confirm or deny the validity. Do not make ANY donation to ANYONE if you haven't confirmed with me that the request is legitimate."

 Good luck!

author: @DaremoKamen
text: Had a couple of ideas I wanted to throw out. For making a small cylindrical coil, what if you wrapped your cylinder with aluminum foil insulation sealing tape for as many layers as you want, then make a series of cuts through the foil tape to the cylinder, half the cuts being from the top of the cylinder but stopping before you reach the bottom, and the other half of the cuts being from the bottom of the cylinder but stopping before you reach the top?  Something like i!i!i! but leaving out the dots.  The other idea is a laminated core for small electromagnets, what if you used ordinary staples? They come out of the box as a laminated stack, it is the stapler that peels them off one at a time as you are stapling.

author: @kennethbain4290
text:
Once upon a time, a few years ago, I remember reading that neurologists had discovered the existence of a bundle of cells in the brain that existed simply to check the accuracy of thoughts in the prefrontal cortex - the part of your brain that creates thoughts and ideas.🤔This is needed because memory is not like a camera, as people tend to think, it is more like a narrator, that changes with shifts in emotions, experiences and moods, etc. It is the biological equivalent of an online spell-checker.
It instantly became a lot easier for me to understand human nature.  Because once you realise that such an organ exists, and that therefore it may also be faulty and not work in some people, hey presto ! Now I understand !🤗
(Of course, I might be lying here...! 😁😜🤯)

replies.text: Erm??? Can you make a channel with a telephone number as your name with a message too ? So it's obvious it's someone at yt doing it

replies.author: @ricksanchez3176
replies.text: @jondoe7936 I've seen some that are not so obvious. Some even start wit a shell account thanking them, then the main poser replying, then back and forth with other shells adding to the conversation. (Yes i do mean shell not shil in this case)

author: @Dalorath
text: You are a good man, I love watching your videos, and I appreciate your positivity and honesty 😀

author: @Dalorath
text: Scammers are Garbage people....  Ive threated many and been involved in the scammer catching network for years....  It feels good to defeat these Donkeys sometimes....

text: Maybe this is a good frontline for AI to look for and accumulate evidence to help in prosecuting these people and make an example out of them. Put their faces up on national news with brief stories of the persons life of crime. Out them, if you will.

author: @mountiedm
text:
Alert!! This is an alert!!

I'm not one for conspiracies, but have you ever looked at the permissions on your phone in detail?? It's absurd.

Oh. And go look up the "back channel protocol" in the IBIS specification.

author: @offgridwanabe
text: This has happens on so many channels I get at least one a week almost like spam email.

text: It´s called the "Dum-dum" tax.

Yep,

author: @dwaynewladyka577
text: Why isn't YouTube doing something about this? It's becoming more prolific. Thanks for sharing this. Cheers!

replies.author: @qmangillett
replies.text: YouTube doesn't care, as long as you watch videos and the ads in-between they get paid. Whether you get scammed in the process or not means literally nothing to them.

replies.author: @dwaynewladyka577
replies.text: @qmangillett YouTube representatives should be doing something about it. It's not a good thing.

replies.author: @aleksandarpetrovic6613
replies.text: @qmangillett Youtube delete videos with truth, not scammers.

replies.author: @qmangillett
replies.text: @dwaynewladyka577 I agree. But realistically they are not going to because they see it as a waste of time/money.

replies.author: @mouse_mc
replies.text: If YouTube/Google use their charity money on something like human trafficking, they can actually make a difference, but they don't. They rather waste that on other things that have a better influence on their PR and hence profits.

author: @Richurd2338
text:
This happened on Sarah Dawn Moore‘s channel as well.They stole her picture and they are slick and by the way they don’t stop at the delivery charge of 100 and some odd dollars.

The next thing they want is 200 for something else shipping label so I’ve heard and also then one more thing “customs charge” by the time you’re done with that you realize that everything doubles.

And if you push back they try to get another $150 out of you and they want you to get steam cards which are I guess for gaming and then you you take a picture of them with the security code scratched off and you sent the picture of the money disappears so I’m told you try to back out of it and it goes oh no I’m really the “so this is my official channel and you’re one of my prize winners.

It’s total BS!

It’s amazing how many people out there are actually so evil that they’ll stoop to this kind of way to make money off of trusting individuals.

There needs to be a special place in hell for them in my opinion!

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: There's an Italian comic bass guitarist on YouTube called Davie504 who had a similar scammer on his channel.  He did a video on a reverse sting he did on the scammer.  Recommend the video, and his channel if his humor strikes you.  Good stuff, and the reverse sting looked easy to pull off.

author: @industrialmonk
text:
I received this message earlier today and immediately reported it to YouTube.
I am a little anti positive & sceptical of any free offers.

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
"...hold them in three inches of water until they stop struggling or just not fall for it... "
Anyone care to ask my preferential treatment for that type? 

Just in case it wasn't clear, it involves a few inches of water, a soft cloth about the face and a resolution involving worms.

author: @Rouverius
text: Yeah, sadly, I've gotten a quite a few of those scams before.  I click on the three dots to the right of those comments and select "Report" I then mark them as "Unwanted commercial content or spam" and click "Report" again... Done and dusted.

author: @murlocchieftain5176
text: Yeah this has been happening everywhere. It's a sad thing

Yes it has. IMO 👇
If anyone falls for the Free stuff that you get once you pay then good for them.
